concerns.text: T026 (FDR correction) is marked complete but depends on T025 (permutation p-values) which is incomplete. FR-006 requires p-values to exist before FDR can be applied. This is a hard producer-consumer violation: the consumer (T026) is listed as complete before its producer (T025) exists.

T035 (load_external.py) depends on T035a (curate external dataset), but T035a is incomplete. The external dataset artifact (kr_cnt.csv) is the producer for T035. Without T035a, T035 cannot execute, breaking the dependency chain for SC-002/SC-003 validation.

T010 (pytest configuration) is incomplete, yet T012, T013, T018, T019, T028, T029 (all test tasks) are marked complete. Tests cannot run or be validated without the pytest configuration and directory structure defined in T010. This violates the foundational-before-consumer rule.

T008 (base data classes) is incomplete, but T014 (descriptors.py) and T015 (preprocess.py) are marked complete. The data classes are the foundational schema for the entities (Adsorbate, Adsorbent) consumed by the descriptor and preprocessing logic. Implementation cannot precede the data model definition.

T034 (diagnostic report for R² < 0.5) is incomplete, but the spec's Edge Cases section explicitly requires this behavior. While not a hard ordering violation, the missing task creates a gap in the 'independently shippable' unit for US3, as the failure mode is unhandled.
